current_fact.fact_text: On April 4, 1581, after circling the globe, Francis Drake knelt aboard his ship Golden Hind as Queen Elizabeth I personally knighted him. His circumnavigation blurred the line between explorer, privateer, and state-sponsored pirate in an age when maps still had blank edges.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - Sailing With Germans
episode.description: From the deck of a sailing boat, hosted by Yasmin and her special guests. A week at sea with six Germans, each with their own stories, songs, and ways of moving through the waves. From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
songs: Ben Howard - Keep Your Head Up

current_fact.match_reason: Drake’s world-encircling voyage and knighting on a wooden deck echo through an episode literally recorded at sea—modern sailors trading stories where his kind once traced unknown coasts.

queue.fact_text: In 1818, the U.S. Congress formally adopted the now-familiar pattern of 13 red and white stripes and one star for each state on the American flag. The design deliberately froze the stripes as a memory of the original colonies while allowing the stars to multiply as the country expanded.

episode.title: Morning Drops #50 - Postcards from Earth-London
episode.description:
Postcards from Earth-London is a Morning Drops walk through the city Gaia has called home for the past ten years, a place made of sound, memory, and late-night streets. 

Four tracks guide the journey: from iconic landmarks to quieter corners, from romance to reality checks, and back to the reason the city still feels alive: music.

queue.match_reason: A flag is a visual postcard from a nation—fixed symbols, shifting meanings; the episode’s city-walk 'postcards' mirror how places and identities are stitched from memory and design.

queue.fact_text: On April 4, 1814, Napoleon Bonaparte signed his first abdication, briefly naming his young son Napoleon II 'Emperor of the French' before surrendering unconditionally two days later. An empire that had redrawn Europe collapsed not in a battle, but in a few lines of ink.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description:
In this first broadcast of 2026, Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection. This episode navigates the delicate transition between closing a significant chapter and stepping into the new year. It explores the concept of constancy amidst change, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and the creative sanctuary that is Sedna FM.

The musical selection mirrors this journey, moving from the orchestral depth of changing seasons into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul. It recalls memories of Swiss mountains and unexpected downpours, reminding us that shared optimism can weather any storm.

Take a moment to breathe in the new morning and listen deeply.

Mood: Reflective, Warm, Transitional, Hopeful
Music genre: Classic Rock, Psychedelic Soul, Pop Rock, Soft Rock

queue.match_reason: Napoleon’s abdication is a storm breaking over Europe and then slowly clearing; the episode’s theme of endings, transitions, and 'it can’t rain forever' resonates with an empire’s abrupt comedown.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1904, a pair of massive earthquakes, each around magnitude 7.1, struck Bulgaria’s Kresna region, among the strongest ever recorded in Europe. Entire villages were shaken apart, reminding people that even an old continent rests on restless fault lines.

episode.title: Morning Drops #14 - Slow Planet
episode.description: A pause. A quiet return to slower rhythms, sparked by a live set under the summer sky. A moment to breathe and settle into the week.

queue.match_reason: Violent seismic jolts contrast with the episode’s intentionally slow, grounding rhythms—a sonic antidote to tectonic chaos, inviting listeners to feel the planet’s quieter side.

queue.fact_text: A year later, in 1905, the Kangra Valley in India was struck by a devastating earthquake that killed around 20,000 people. Temples and hill towns crumbled along the foothills of the Himalayas, reshaping both the landscape and the memory of those who lived there.

episode.title: Morning Drops #22 - Sicilians On The Dolomites
episode.description: Hosted by Yasmin, a musical journey inspired by the mountains. Joined by two special guests, exploring the tracks they've chosen, hearing the stories behind their picks, and discovering the personal memories each song brings to life — a blend of music, voices, and landscapes you can hear.
songs: Florian Pellissier Quintet - Coup de foudre à Thessalonique
Lucio Dalla & Francesco De Gregori - Ma come fanno i marinai

queue.match_reason: A Himalayan quake in a mountain valley connects with an episode about living with and within mountains; both explore how high landscapes shape stories, awe, and vulnerability.

queue.fact_text: On April 4, 1933, the U.S. Navy airship USS Akron broke apart in a storm off the New Jersey coast, killing 73 people and ending hopes that giant rigid airships would be safe flagships of the sky. It remains one of the deadliest airship disasters in history, overshadowed only by the later Hindenburg fire.

episode.title: Evening Flows #01 - Yasmin - Crescendo in the Medina
episode.description: The first Evening Flow episode comes from Yasmin. It starts deep in the medina, slow, mysterious, textured, and grows into a liberating dance across sub-Saharan landscapes. A journey through ambient soundscapes, Arabic melodies, Afro-Latin rhythms, and funky highlife grooves.
songs: David Visan & Carlos Campos - Crystal Heart
Evan Mars - Ya Zareef Al Tool
Dele Sosimi & Medlar - You No Fit Touch Am (Medlar Remix)

queue.match_reason: The Akron’s doomed voyage evokes the fragile romance of early flight; the episode’s slowly rising, airy textures and 'solar wind' ambience echo that sense of drifting high above the Earth.

queue.fact_text: On April 4, 1944, Allied bombers targeted oil refineries around Bucharest, Romania, in a raid that killed around 3,000 civilians. The attack underscored how modern war depended on fuel—and how entire cities could be burned in the effort to deny it.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

queue.match_reason: A brutal wartime air campaign and the politics of power and resources resonate with Rage Against the Machine’s fierce critique of militarism and imperial violence.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1958, the now-iconic CND peace symbol—a circle with a semaphore 'N' and 'D' for nuclear disarmament—was displayed in public for the first time at a march in London. Designed by Gerald Holtom, it was meant as a symbol of despair, but quickly became a worldwide emblem of hope and protest.

episode.title: Morning Drops #04 - Bars With Balls
episode.description: Yasmin dives into Women's Day through the voices of four incredible female rappers. Morning fire from the magic planet of Sedna FM — move your hands up and down with us. Hip hop culture through a feminine lens.
songs: Little Simz - Point and Kill (feat. Obongjayar)

queue.match_reason: The birth of a protest symbol for peace lines up with an episode centered on hip-hop as a voice for justice and empowerment—both turn art and symbols into political tools.

queue.fact_text: On April 4, 1969, heart surgeon Denton Cooley implanted the first temporary artificial heart in a human patient, using it as a bridge until a donor heart became available. For three days, a mechanical pump took over the work of a human heart, turning science fiction into surgery.

episode.title: Sedna FM #06 - Alters of an Ego
episode.description: What if you could step outside yourself and become someone freer, bolder, or completely unexpected? Exploring the artistry of musicians who craft alter-egos and parallel realities to experiment with new dimensions of their identity. It's not about hiding who they are — it's about expanding who they can be.
songs: The Fireman - Strawberries Oceans Ships Forest

queue.match_reason: A mechanical device stepping in as a temporary 'second heart' feels like a bodily alter-ego; the episode’s exploration of parallel selves and alternate identities mirrors this radical substitution.

queue.fact_text: On April 4, 1973, a Lockheed C-141 nicknamed the 'Hanoi Taxi' made the final flight of Operation Homecoming, bringing American prisoners of war back from North Vietnam. The aircraft, once a symbol of conflict, became an airborne doorway back to families, streets, and lives put on pause.

episode.title: Morning Drops #06 - Friend's Home
episode.description: Yasmin and Gaia are reunited under the same roof to spend Easter Monday morning together, exploring that heartwarming 'just like home' feeling that comes from being surrounded by beloved friends.

queue.match_reason: An aircraft named 'Hanoi Taxi' carrying people home after years of captivity resonates with an episode about the warmth and relief of finally being under a friendly roof again.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1975, a U.S. Air Force C-5A Galaxy carrying Vietnamese orphans and caregivers as part of Operation Babylift crashed shortly after takeoff from near Saigon, killing 172 people. A mission meant to rescue children from war ended as one of the deadliest aviation disasters of the conflict.

episode.title: Morning Drops #43 - Looking Back
episode.description:
In this first Morning Drop of 2026, Yasmin opens a space for quiet reflection on the nature of transitions. The episode traces a personal journey that began three years ago, when a sudden injury on New Year’s Day turned a desire for movement into a mandatory season of stillness.

Through the story of a broken knee and the slow process of relearning to walk, the narrative explores the difference between being strong and being aware. It touches on the vulnerability of the body, the unexpected joy found in daily progress, and the warmth that comes from finally allowing oneself to accept help. The musical selection mirrors this path of recovery, moving from the uncertainty of change to the steady rhythm of healing.

Mood: Reflective, Resilient, Grounded
Music genre: Indie Pop, Cinematic, World, Soul

We invite you to listen deeply and find comfort in your own pace.
songs: Kelly Finnigan - I Don't Wanna Wait
Goran Bregović & Iggy Pop - This Is a Film

queue.match_reason: The tragedy of Operation Babylift invites somber reflection on intentions, fragility, and loss; the episode’s meditative look back on pain and slow healing provides an emotional framework to hold such a story.

queue.fact_text: On April 4, 2010, a magnitude 7.2 earthquake struck near the Mexico–United States border in Baja California, cracking roads like ribbons and swaying buildings on both sides. The quake reminded two countries that beneath borders and politics, they share the same restless crust.

episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by how travel helps us see more clearly. Stepping into new places opens the mind, shifts perspective. The music moves through balearic warmth, boogie grooves, world and latin-folk influences, and touches of new wave — a selection built to follow the quiet changes that happen when you're out of your usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto
Talking Heads - This Must Be the Place (Naive Melody)

queue.match_reason: A cross-border earthquake literally shifts ground between two countries; the episode’s theme of movement, travel, and perspective shifts offers a gentler, introspective counterpart to that sudden jolt.

queue.fact_text: On April 4, 2023, Finland formally became a member of NATO, ending decades of military non-alignment. A nation known for its long, quiet border with Russia suddenly rewrote its security story in response to a changing European landscape.

episode.title: Morning Drops #28 - Under Scottish Skies
episode.description: Gaia shares a personal moment of transition — a shift in seasons, in rhythm, and in life. From this space of quiet change comes a musical journey through Scotland: not the usual names, but voices that speak from the edges. A personal ritual and a small act of curiosity: an invitation to notice what grounds us when everything moves.
songs: Paolo Nutini - Pencil Full of Lead

queue.match_reason: Finland’s NATO entry is a geopolitical change under northern skies; the episode’s reflection on shifting seasons, identity, and what grounds us fits a quiet but historic realignment in Europe’s north.

queue.fact_text: On April 4, 1904, the same Kresna region in Bulgaria felt twin earthquakes, but long afterward, scientists used their patterns to study how faults rupture in sequence. The quakes became a natural laboratory for understanding how the Earth sometimes shakes not once, but in unsettling pairs.

episode.title: Morning Drops #19 - Wabi Sabi
episode.description: Wabi-sabi is a story about a handmade mug, a small mistake, and the idea that imperfection can be something to keep—not correct. In this Morning Drop, Gaia shares a moment that didn’t go to plan, and four tracks that echo the same spirit: rough takes, studio accidents, lo-fi textures, and quiet surprises. A soft, thoughtful selection moving between warm electronics, classic riffs, and raw edges.A reminder that not everything needs to be polished. Sometimes, it’s better that way.

queue.match_reason: Twin quakes and their irregular ruptures embody nature’s rough edges and unpredictability; the episode’s embrace of imperfection and 'happy accidents' mirrors that unpolished seismic reality.

queue.fact_text: On April 4, 1963, 'Bye Bye Birdie' hit cinemas with playful choreography and swooning teens, but it also quietly documented the birth of television as a cultural megaphone. A fictional rock idol’s staged farewell kiss showed how screens could orchestrate emotions far beyond the studio walls.

episode.title: Morning Drops #47 - The Rise Of Autotune
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin explores the explosive rise of Auto-Tune, a tool that quietly changed the sound of music without asking for attention. From fragile, imperfect recordings to voices shaped with precision, this episode follows the moment when correction became standard and sound became controlled.

Along the way, we discover the curious story of how it was invented, how it moved from the background of studios to the center of debate, and how a few artists turned it into something expressive rather than hidden.

Listen slowly, and let us know what you think about Autotune.
songs: Chet Baker – I Fall in Love Too Easily

queue.match_reason: A film satirizing the mediation of pop stardom through TV aligns with an episode on a technology—Auto-Tune—that equally mediates and reshapes how we experience the pop voice.

queue.fact_text: On April 4, 1972, long before its crash, the colossal C-5 Galaxy used in Operation Babylift symbolized a new scale of global airlift—planes big enough to carry tanks or whole hospital wards. By the mid-1970s, its role in both logistics and tragedy showed how technology can magnify both rescue and risk.

episode.title: Sedna FM #05 - On The Go - Zero Zero
episode.description: An energetic mix for when you're on the move. Zero Zero captures that feeling of starting fresh, moving forward, and embracing the journey. Perfect for travel, walks, or just getting things done.
songs: Grace Jones - Pull up to the bumper
Easy star all star - Soul love
James Brown - Talking loud and saying nothing (remix)

queue.match_reason: A giant transport aircraft built to move entire worlds of cargo fits with an episode about being 'on the go'—motion, journeys, and the energy of starting fresh and moving things forward.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1972, early work on what became the NEAR Shoemaker mission began to redefine how we visit small worlds, eventually leading to that camera turning toward comet Hyakutake in 1996. It marked a subtle shift: from visiting planets to chasing asteroids and comets, treating even the smallest rocks as destinations.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

queue.match_reason: A probe redefining 'destination' in space—treating asteroids and comets as places, not specks—beautifully echoes an episode about setting unusual destinations, like Sedna, for shared sonic voyages.

published.fact_text: On April 4, 1983, Space Shuttle Challenger made its maiden voyage on mission STS-6, carrying a crew of four and deploying the first Tracking and Data Relay Satellite. It was only the second orbital flight of the Shuttle program to use the giant solid rocket boosters that defined its dramatic launches.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: A literal 'space shift': Challenger’s first leap into orbit pairs with an episode about turning empty space into possibility, backed by forward-driving, skyward-feeling tracks.

published.fact_text: In 1968, NASA launched Apollo 6, the second and last uncrewed test of the mighty Saturn V rocket. The mission suffered serious engine problems yet still reached orbit, proving the rocket’s resilience just months before humans would ride it toward the Moon.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

published.match_reason: The raw power and engine failures of Apollo 6 feel like a cosmic 'electric shock'—loud, volatile, and transformative—matching the episode’s high-voltage rock energy.

published.fact_text: On April 4, 1997, Space Shuttle Columbia launched on STS-83, a mission meant to be a long microgravity science lab. A failing fuel cell forced NASA to cut it short after just a few days, turning a carefully planned experiment into a lesson in contingency and resilience.

episode.title: Morning Drops #42 - The Joy Of Learning
episode.description:
Hosted by Yasmin, this Morning Drop explores learning as a lifelong process, focusing on persistence, repetition, and the emotional landscape that comes with challenging oneself over time.

Through personal stories and scientific insights, the episode looks at how learning reshapes the brain, generates joy through effort, and why progress often arrives after confusion and frustration. It reflects on the so-called delayed pleasure effect, the role of dopamine in motivation, and how curiosity and learning support long-term wellbeing.

The episode also questions our cultural relationship with failure and invites a softer, more realistic approach to learning, one that accepts mistakes as part of growth and sees persistence as a quiet form of strength.

Mood: reflective, grounded, quietly hopeful
Music genre: alternative rock, hip hop, electronic, neo-soul, drill

A space to listen slowly and stay with the process.

published.match_reason: STS-83’s aborted mission embodies learning through failure and iteration; the episode dives into exactly that mindset—finding growth when plans break.

published.fact_text: On this day in 1996, comet Hyakutake—one of the closest cometary visitors in centuries—was imaged by the NEAR Shoemaker spacecraft. For a brief moment, a mission designed for asteroids turned its camera toward an icy wanderer flashing through the inner Solar System.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Yasmin's friend Caroline takes us beyond the polar circle, into the Arctic waters of Norway, where orcas and humpback whales gather each winter. A personal journey shaped by a quiet obsession with orcas, from Patagonia to the icy fjords of the north. The soundscape moves gently between cinematic swells and soft, rhythmic pulses — echoing the cold light of Arctic mornings and the grace of underwater encounters.

published.match_reason: A cold, fleeting visitor from deep space mirrors seasonal orcas in icy fjords—both rare, graceful encounters in the dark blue; the episode’s cinematic calm fits Hyakutake’s ghostly pass.

published.fact_text: In 1963, the film 'Bye Bye Birdie' premiered, translating the Broadway musical’s satire of rock ’n’ roll hysteria into technicolor cinema. Loosely inspired by Elvis Presley’s draft, it captured how pop idols can turn small towns into stages.

episode.title: Morning Drops #51 - The Dancefloor
episode.description:
For International Women’s Day, this Morning Drop turns its focus to women on the dancefloor, not as a trend, but as a cultural force.

Across five tracks, Gaia follows a line from disco closers to hip hop club classics, tracing how the dancefloor became a place where women could take space, build confidence, and create connection, sometimes with friends, sometimes alone, always with music at the centre.

This episode isn’t about pointing fingers. It’s a gentle reminder that spaces like the dancefloor still matter: places where people can express themselves, meet each other with respect, and feel part of something bigger than a night out.

Tracklist:
Donna Summer - Last Dance (1978)
The Sequence  - Funk you up
Salt-N-Pepa - Push It
Queen Latifah - Ladies First
Alicia Bridges - I Love the Nightlife

published.match_reason: A movie about pop mania and TV performances leading to communal dancing pairs with an episode that treats the dancefloor itself as a cultural, liberating stage.

published.fact_text: On April 4, 1964, The Beatles did something no band has repeated: they occupied all top five spots on the Billboard Hot 100. For one surreal week, American pop charts read like a single-songbook from Liverpool.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - secret_mp3_player_1
episode.description: Inspired by a throwback MP3 player on Instagram, Yasmin and Gaia take you on a nostalgic journey to the early 2000s. LimeWire, eMule, guilty-pleasure playlists — remember those? Hit play and relive the songs that shaped a generation.

published.match_reason: Chart-dominating Beatles for one generation reflect the ubiquitous pop of early-2000s MP3 culture; both moments define what 'everywhere' music sounds like to a youth generation.

published.fact_text: In 1796, French naturalist Georges Cuvier delivered what is considered the first formal paleontology lecture. By comparing fossils with living animals, he argued that entire species had vanished, quietly introducing the idea of extinction to a world that thought nature never erased her own work.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - A Trip-Hop Journey
episode.description: Hosted by friend Lorenzo, a journey through the roots and evolution of trip-hop. From Bristol's early experiments with Massive Attack and Tricky, to DJ Shadow's cinematic sampling in California, and Thievery Corporation's global downtempo grooves in Washington D.C. — exploring how this genre blurred boundaries and reshaped electronic music.
songs: Tricky - Hell Is Round the Corner
DJ Shadow - Midnight in a Perfect World

published.match_reason: Cuvier stitched Earth’s deep past together from fragments, much like trip-hop stitches samples into new soundscapes—both are acts of reconstructing hidden histories from scattered traces.

published.fact_text: On April 4, 1581, after circling the globe, Francis Drake knelt aboard his ship Golden Hind as Queen Elizabeth I personally knighted him. His circumnavigation blurred the line between explorer, privateer, and state-sponsored pirate in an age when maps still had blank edges.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - Sailing With Germans
episode.description: From the deck of a sailing boat, hosted by Yasmin and her special guests. A week at sea with six Germans, each with their own stories, songs, and ways of moving through the waves. From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
songs: Ben Howard - Keep Your Head Up

published.match_reason: Drake’s world-encircling voyage and knighting on a wooden deck echo through an episode literally recorded at sea—modern sailors trading stories where his kind once traced unknown coasts.
